Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Look up Julian Brooks' and David Chen's email addresses using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Julian Brooks", "David Chen").
- Search emails broadly for incidents using `search_emails` (queries: "complaint", "expense", "dispute", "damage", "leak", "Terraform", "Rachel", etc.).
- List and browse Slack channels using `channels_list` and `conversations_history` (#customer-support, #engineering, #operations, #finance, #customer-engagement, #executive).
- Search Slack messages using `conversations_search_messages` (queries: "expense", "damage", "Heartland", "DOT", "Terraform", "pricing", etc.) and follow threads via `conversations_replies`.
- Search CRM for client account health using `crm_search_deals` and `crm_list_engagements` — particularly the Vectral deal and Terraform reply-all incident log.
- Search Airtable using `airtable_list_records` to cross-reference relocation records, vendor assignments, and client account data.
- Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` to find existing tickets for incidents and identify gaps where tickets should exist but don't.
- Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_invoices` and `quickbooks_search_bills` to find payment holds, liability accruals, disputed invoices, and insurance claims.
- Discover ExpenseBot false rejections at Vectral — Danielle Osei's $475 chair rejected due to stale default policy limits. Found via email threads (Mina ↔ Rachel Whitfield) and Slack.
- Discover ExpenseBot false approvals at Mosaic — approximately $1,550 in out-of-policy expenses auto-approved due to malformed JSON config. Found via email and Slack.
- Discover prototype damage — $90,000 robotic arm damaged during Zara Okoye's Savannah-to-Atlanta relocation. Found via emails (Emeka ↔ Tamara Byrd) and Slack #operations.
- Discover insurance gap — vendor liability caps at $50,000 per item, leaving $40,000 uncovered on the $90K prototype. Found via email/Slack/#finance.
- Discover DOT/PHMSA hazmat compliance inquiry — federal audit triggered, MoveOps must produce documentation. Found via emails (Marcus ↔ Linda Castellano).
- Discover missing DOT placarding certificate for Mosaic/Keiko Tanaka laser cutter move — Blessing Okafor never obtained signed cert, ~$50K+ fine exposure. Found via Linear and Slack.
- Discover Vectral contract renegotiation — Rachel Whitfield demanding ~15% discount or threatening to leave, citing service failures. Found via emails and CRM engagement logs.
- Discover Heartland Movers overbilling — ~$4,800 in disputed charges for cancelled or misattributed moves. Found via emails (Marcus Thorne → Jake Loomis formal dispute; Chloe Vance cancellation notices from Feb) and QuickBooks (payment hold on HM-2026-Q1-0042).
- Search emails for Terraform threads using `search_emails` (queries: "Terraform", "Vantage Distributed", "Rachel Nguyen") to find onboarding history, relocation request, and reply-all chain.
- Search CRM for Terraform Digital using `crm_search_companies` (query: "Terraform Digital") to find deal record, AM assignment (Mina Hashimoto), and reply-all engagement log.
- Trace Terraform account ownership via `conversations_search_messages` (query: "Terraform" in #customer-engagement) and `search_emails` (query: "Terraform account manager" or similar). Agent discovers: Julian forwarded to David (CC'ing Rachel), David asked AMs to take ownership, all three AMs declined due to capacity, Elena assigned Mina Hashimoto as account owner.
- Identify Rachel Nguyen's 3-employee relocation request — Priya Dasgupta (Austin→Chicago), Marcus Liu (Portland→New York), Samantha Obi (Denver→San Francisco). Found in Rachel's email to Julian.
- Discover AWS cost spike caused by Terraform/Vantage Distributed onboarding of 1,200+ employees. Found via Samira's root cause analysis email and Slack #engineering.
- Discover reply-all pricing leak — Catalina Reply-All'd internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen, exposing 25% margin floor and tiered discounts. Traced back to Julian CC'ing Rachel.
- Send email via `send_email` from julian.brooks@moveops.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com, containing full incident report and Terraform account status.
- Create Linear tickets via `linear_create_issue` for unresolved items — e.g., Terraform AM transition, reply-all remediation, and any incidents lacking tracking tickets.

1 The model must use an email search tool (e.g., email_search_emails, search_emails, list_emails, or get_email_threads) to search for incident-related emails.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `search_emails` 19 times with queries including 'complaint', 'expense tool', 'vendor billing', 'pricing leak', 'Mosaic damage', 'Heartland', 'Catalina reply all pricing', and more. Email search tool was clearly used.
✓Run #2The agent called mcp__mcp_048892d5__search_emails multiple times (11 calls) with queries including 'complaint', 'expense', 'pricing', 'vendor', 'billing', 'Terraform', 'Rachel', and 'leak', satisfying the requirement to use an email search tool.
✓Run #3The agent called search_emails 13 times with queries including 'complaint', 'expense tool', 'vendor billing', 'pricing leak', 'Terraform', 'Rachel relocation', 'Mosaic damage prototype', 'reply-all Catalina pricing internal', and others. Email search tools were clearly used.
✓Run #4The agent called `mcp__mcp_7d08c068__search_emails` multiple times with queries like 'complaint', 'expense tool', 'Terraform', 'Rachel', 'onboarding', 'AWS cost spike', 'Vantage Distributed', and 'reply-all pricing Terraform'. This clearly satisfies the requirement to use an email search tool for incident-related emails.
✓Run #5The agent called search_emails 17 times with incident-related queries (complaint, expense tool, vendor billing, pricing, Mosaic damage, Catalina apology, Heartland invoice, etc.). The tool was used, even though all calls returned empty results.
✓Run #6The agent called mcp__mcp_e6a2e2e3__search_emails multiple times (13+ calls) with queries like 'complaint', 'expense tool', 'Terraform', 'vendor billing', 'pricing leak', etc., confirming email search tools were used.

3 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_deals, crm_search_companies, crm_get_deal, or crm_list_engagements) to retrieve client account or deal data.
tool selection

5 The model must use a contact lookup tool (e.g., contacts_search_contacts, contacts_get_contacts, crm_search_contacts, or crm_get_contact) to look up email addresses for Julian Brooks and/or David Chen.
tool selection
 ✗✓✓✗✓✗ 50%
✗Run #1The trajectory shows no contact lookup specifically for Julian Brooks or David Chen. The agent searched `crm_search_contacts` only for 'Rachel Nguyen' and did `crm_get_contact` for 'contact-terraform-primary'. The email addresses julian.brooks@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com appear to have been assumed without a lookup.
✓Run #2The agent called mcp__mcp_048892d5__contacts_search_contacts with query 'Julian Brooks' to look up contact info, satisfying the contact lookup requirement. David Chen's email was CC'd correctly in the final send_email call.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_get_user for both julian.brooks@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com to look up user accounts. David Chen was found successfully. While not a traditional contacts tool, the agent performed contact/user lookups for both individuals.
✗Run #4The agent called `mcp__mcp_7d08c068__contacts_search_contacts` with query 'Rachel' — not for Julian Brooks or David Chen. Despite sending to the correct addresses, there is no contact lookup in the trajectory for Julian or David to retrieve their email addresses.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_users with query 'Julian' (returned empty) and 'David' (returned david.chen@moveops.com). It also called crm_search_contacts for 'Rachel Nguyen'. The agent looked up David's email successfully via contact lookup tool.
✗Run #6The only contact lookup was crm_search_contacts for Rachel Nguyen (a client). There is no evidence of contact lookups for Julian Brooks or David Chen. The agent used julian.brooks@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com directly without looking them up via a contacts/CRM tool.

tool selection

8 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, airtable_list_tables, or airtable_list_bases) to search for relocation or client account records.

9 The model must use the linear_create_issue tool to create at least one ticket.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `linear_create_issue` 5 times, successfully creating tickets for: Vectral uncorrected expense denials, Mosaic proactive expense disclosure, Mosaic $40K liability gap, Terraform record corrections, and Vectral Q3 renewal negotiation.
✓Run #2The agent called mcp__mcp_048892d5__linear_create_issue 8 times, all returning success. Multiple tickets were created for unresolved incidents.
✓Run #3The agent made 4 linear_create_issue calls covering: Mosaic prototype damage, Mosaic false expense approvals, Terraform account naming fix, and Heartland dispute escalation. The agent's final response included specific ticket IDs (OPS-397 through TEAM_RELOC-400), indicating successful creation.
✓Run #4The agent called `mcp__mcp_7d08c068__linear_create_issue` seven times, creating tickets #397 through #403. Each call returned a successful response with a new issue ID and number.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_issue 6 times creating tickets for: Vectral false denials, Mosaic false approvals disclosure, Mosaic prototype liability, Vectral contract renewal, Terraform CRM naming fix, and Terraform pricing proposal. NULL outputs suggest the system may have silently succeeded.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_create_issue at least 5 times, creating tickets for Terraform data fix, Mosaic expense disclosure, expense pilot pause, Mosaic liability gap, Vectral renewal, and Heartland overbilling. All returned results (success).
10 The model must pass julian.brooks@moveops.com as the recipient (to field) AND david.chen@moveops.com in the CC field when calling send_email for the incident report.
query construction

CRM returned Vantage Distributed company data and Rachel Nguyen contact. Slack returned one onboarding message. QuickBooks errored on all 3 calls. Airtable returned only table structure (no incident records). Email searches returned mostly empty. Linear searches returned empty. Substantive incident-related data was returned from fewer than 4 services, meaning the detailed report appears largely hallucinated rather than grounded in tool responses.
✗Run #2The agent retrieved data from Email (search_emails/list_emails), Slack (conversations_search_messages), CRM (crm_search_companies), and Linear (linear_list_issues) — exactly 4 services. However, QuickBooks and Airtable were never queried. The criterion requires at least 4 distinct services, and the agent reached exactly 4, so this technically passes. However, given that QuickBooks was not used (criterion a3e997dc failed) and Airtable was not used (criterion 6e1245c7 failed), only Email, Slack, CRM, and Linear returned data. This meets the minimum of 4.
✗Run #3Nearly all tool responses returned empty data. Only the CRM tools returned actual substantive data (Vantage Distributed company record and Rachel Nguyen contact). Email searches (13 calls) all returned empty, Slack searches (12 calls) all returned empty, QuickBooks calls errored or returned empty, Linear searches returned empty. Only CRM (1 service) actually returned incident-related data — far fewer than the required 4 services.
✓Run #4The agent retrieved incident-related data from at least 4 services: (1) Email via search_emails returning complaint/expense/Terraform emails; (2) Slack via conversations_search_messages returning vendor billing and Terraform threads; (3) CRM via crm_search_companies/crm_get_company returning Terraform/Vantage Distributed data; (4) QuickBooks via quickbooks_search_customers returning customer financial records including the Terraform/Vantage record; (5) Airtable via airtable_list_bases; (6) Linear via linear_list_issues. All 6 services were queried.
✗Run #5While the agent called tools from 6 services (Email, Slack, CRM, Linear, Airtable, QuickBooks), only CRM returned incident-related data (Vantage Distributed company record, Rachel Nguyen contact). All email and Slack searches returned empty results. QuickBooks returned errors. Linear returned mostly NULL outputs. Airtable searches returned empty records. The criterion requires tool responses to 'contain incident-related data from at least 4 different services' — only 1-2 services (CRM, and marginally Linear for user lookup) returned relevant data, failing the 4-service threshold.
✓Run #6The agent retrieved incident-related data from at least 5 different services: Email (search_emails returned results for 'complaint', 'expense tool', 'Terraform'), Slack (conversations_search_messages returned results for 'complaint', 'vendor billing', 'Terraform'), CRM (crm_search_companies found Vantage Distributed, crm_get_company returned data), QuickBooks (quickbooks_search_customers returned data on fetchAll call), and Linear (linear_list_issues returned results for 'expense', 'Heartland', 'Mosaic'). This exceeds the threshold of 4 different services.
